new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in containers > 2 L – other (subheading 2204 29 38)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.) in Behältnissen > 2 L – andere (Unterposition 2204 29 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO) within the meaning of additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, not elsewhere specified under subheading 2204 29.

coverage_explanation: The two main distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) the wine bears a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), modelled via the existing ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality quality class; and (2) it is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, modelled via the existing LargeContainer part restriction. A full coverage gap remains because the legal text cross-references "additional note 6(a) to this chapter", which provides detailed definitional criteria for PDO wines (e.g. specific geographic scope, production rules, registration requirements). Those criteria are contained in a note not supplied in the input, so they cannot be axiomatised here, and the quality class used (PDO/PGI combined) is slightly broader than the strictly PDO-only scope of this subheading. Additionally, 2204 29 38 sits within the range 22042922–22042978 covering PDO wines in containers > 2 L, but the further internal sub-divisions (e.g. alcohol strength bands that differentiate 22042922, 22042928, 22042932, 22042938) are not reflected in the available legal text fragment, preventing complete discrimination from sibling codes.
